title: KeyId and NvmId are pub newtypes with pub inner field, From<u16> both ways
description: KeyId(pub u16) and NvmId(pub u16) are newtype structs with public inner fields and bidirectional From<u16>/From<KeyId/NvmId> conversions. The pub inner field is a deliberate escape hatch: downstream code that needs to pass the raw u16 to other C bindings or serialize it can do so without a separate accessor. Both types also carry ERASED/INVALID constants mirroring the C sentinel values. A future contributor might argue the inner field should be private (pub(crate)) for encapsulation, but given the C FFI context — where callers routinely need to pass raw IDs to other wolfSSL APIs not wrapped here — forced accessor verbosity would not add safety and would reduce usability.
